title: User-defined column can conflict with internally generated subcolumn
description:
### Company or project name
In MergeTree tables, some column types internally generate subcolumns (e.g., Array columns generate `size0`). If a user manually creates a column with the same name as one of these internal subcolumns, it can lead to conflicts.
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
* **Compact parts** Compact parts handle this by a rule where user-defined columns override internally generated subcolumns, so queries do not fail. However, this breaks `optimize_functions_to_subcolumns`. For example, `length(a)` may read the user-defined column instead of the actual Array length.
* **Wide parts** can fail during deserialization, causing queries depending on the column or subcolumn to break.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Master
### How to reproduce
**Example using Array:**
```sql
-- Compact part works by overriding internally generated subcolumns
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS a;
CREATE TABLE a(a Array(Int), `a.size0` UInt64) ENGINE MergeTree ORDER BY ()
SETTINGS min_bytes_for_wide_part = 100000;
INSERT INTO a VALUES ([123, 234], 1);
SELECT a, a.size0, length(a) FROM a
SETTINGS optimize_functions_to_subcolumns = 0;
DROP TABLE a;
-- Wide part fails
CREATE TABLE a(a Array(Int), `a.size0` UInt64) ENGINE MergeTree ORDER BY ()
SETTINGS min_bytes_for_wide_part = 0;
INSERT INTO a VALUES ([123, 234], 1);
-- Deserialization fails due to conflict between manual column and internal size0
SELECT a, a.size0, length(a) FROM a
SETTINGS optimize_functions_to_subcolumns = 0;
DROP TABLE a;
```
### Expected behavior
Wide parts should follow the same rule as compact parts: user-defined columns override internally generated subcolumns without breaking deserialization. Also, functions like length(a) should reflect the actual Array length, even if a user-defined subcolumn exists.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(a TEXT, a_size0 INTEGER);
SELECT a, a_size0, LENGTH(a) FROM test_table;
key_bug_features: User-defined column conflicts with internal subcolumn names
